_comment: Source-of-truth GitHub Repository Ruleset for the master branch of
Azure/azure-quickstart-templates.
GitHub does NOT natively read this file. To apply changes, a repository
admin must run:
bash .github/scripts/apply-ruleset.sh           # apply
bash .github/scripts/apply-ruleset.sh --dry-run # preview only
Two-phase rollout via the `enforcement` field below:
Phase 1 (current): "evaluate" — dry-run mode. Rules are checked and
would-be violations are logged to the ruleset Insights page in the
GitHub UI, but NOTHING is blocked. Use this to confirm both
required checks land on the head SHA across a handful of real PRs
before turning enforcement on.
Phase 2 (promotion): an admin edits this file and changes
"enforcement" to "active", merges the change, then re-runs
`bash .github/scripts/apply-ruleset.sh`. From that moment merges
to master are blocked unless every required check is green.
Note: GitHub also accepts "disabled" (ruleset present but inert).
The repository Settings UI only surfaces Active/Disabled; the REST
API used by apply-ruleset.sh accepts all three values.
The required_status_checks below pin checks to integration_id 15368,
which is the GitHub Actions app. This prevents another app from
satisfying the requirement by reporting a check with the same name.
validate-samples-results        - aggregator job in validate-samples.yml
(matrix names cannot be required;
this static name is the gate).
adx-deployment-validation       - check published from
ValidateSampleDeployments.yml when
/validate runs. Pushing a new commit
moves PR head to a new SHA where this
check has not been reported, blocking
merge until /validate is re-run.
name: master-protection
